Review Text: Friendly staff❤️Great show !!❤️
Venue name: Door Shakespeare Day Time Office and Gift Shop

Review Text:
Best theater in Door County.  Among the best Shakespeare performances I've ever seen -- easy to understand and clearly communicates the nuances and opens meaning from the original.

Gorgeous outdoor setting makes the most of Door County's natural beauty.  I still can't get over that tree!  14 minutes from Egg Harbor.  (Try the Wine Night, delicious food and wine and company, thank me later.)
Owner Response Text:
Hello Luther!

Thank you so much for your kind words! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed your time here - and the food and drink, too! We look forward to seeing you at performances to come. :)

Best,
Door Shakespeare
Venue name: Door Shakespeare Day Time Office and Gift Shop

Review Text: Absolutely wonderful production! Our family members were 8-72 and everyone enjoyed the Shakespeare production. All seats are close enough to hear everything, see the action and become totally absorbed in the action. Beautiful costumes and set. Lighting and outdoor atmosphere was great. Don't miss this!
Owner Response Text:
Hello, Nancy!

Delighted to hear that you and your family enjoyed the production! Thanks so much for the kind review. We look forward to seeing you again!
Venue name: Door Shakespeare Day Time Office and Gift Shop

Review Text: My favorite part of Door County!!!! Absolutely amazing stuff. I’ve been going for a while. They blow me away every time!
Owner Response Text: Thank you so much, Jade! We're thrilled to hear you've enjoyed your visits here - we can't wait to have you back soon!
Venue name: Door Shakespeare Day Time Office and Gift Shop
Door Shakespeare Day Time Office and Gift Shop

Review Text:
Recently, attended the Shakespeare play “As you like it”.
Venue is splendid.
Actors performing it were talented.
Artistic presentation ( I.e. costumes, added scenery, etc.) was disappointing.
Actors costumes were lackluster or nonexistent.
The lovely venue could have added some enhancements but I failed to see any.
Venue name: Door Shakespeare Day Time Office and Gift Shop

Review Text: I'd never been to a Shakespheare play before.  I always had such a hard time following the lines and understanding what was being said. This was a completely unique and wonderful experience and I loved it! The actors were able to make the wordiness of Shakeaphere sound natural. I was so impressed with how well the actors actually understood what they were saying and gave such realistic expression and mannerisms that actually helped me to understand and follow along easily. I was absolutely absorbed in the story and the humor. I can't wait to go again. I highly recommend the experience. On a practical side: They have a few consession snacks and drinks, but not regular food so plan accordingly. Dress for outside weather. I didn't need bug spray but you might. The location is hard to find. The GPS took me to the wrong entrance and it took me a while to circle around and find it. There are signs which are very helpful.
Venue name: Door Shakespeare Day Time Office and Gift Shop

Review Text:
This gem, tucked into the forest of Door County, is well worth the visit. We were greeted with a smile as we drove in on where to park and all through the place there's this sense of "may we help you".

The production of "As You Like It" was amazing and you could see the performers feeding off the engagement of the audience.

I highly recommend Door Shakespeare and, if we return to Door County, we'll be back here too!
Venue name: Door Shakespeare Day Time Office and Gift Shop

Review Text: We are in the area and they are difficult neighbors - they complain about everything and are very rude. They badmouth people all the time. We are hearing a lot of grumblings from them - and we like others are sick of it. Go somewhere else - we don't need people like that in our area. We want neighborly people who don't cause havoc on everyone around here. They need to focus on being positive toward the community.
Owner Response Text:
Hi Laura,

I'm looking through our archives and see that this review has not yet been followed up with. We greatly apologize for any poor attitudes you may have encountered from our company here at Door Shakespeare. We are a company made up of some folks that reside here year-round, as well as others who only live here for three months out of the year. No matter how long we're here for, what we all have in common is that we love Door County and the people in it, and the absolute last thing we hope to do is to cause anyone grief--especially our own neighbors. If you would ever like to come in to our daytime box office and speak with us about how we can be better neighbors, we'd be more than willing to listen.

Wishing you all the best,
Door Shakespeare
Venue name: Door Shakespeare Day Time Office and Gift Shop

Review Text: Terrible looking building and driveway - poor presentation.
Owner Response Text:
Hi Tam,

I'm looking through our archives and see that you left us this review in 2022 - I'm sorry to hear that you seem to have caught us when we were just moving in to our new daytime box office. Like many other theaters throughout this country, we often have to prioritize where our funds go, and in the wake of a big move, we had to take our time to chip away at enhancing our new home. That said, I can guarantee that we have made great strides in sprucing up the place in the three years since you've visited, and would love for you to come visit us again! Otherwise, if you're looking for some guaranteed gorgeous views, head south for 8.5 miles on Highway 57--the sight of our outdoor stage under the stars and the maple tree at Bjorklunden is not a sight to be missed. :)

Best,
Door Shakespeare
Venue name: Door Shakespeare Day Time Office and Gift Shop

Review Text: Really fun!  Had a great time!  Fabulous one man show of Hamlet!  Beautiful setting!  Don't miss it! Bravo!
Venue name: Door Shakespeare Day Time Office and Gift Shop

Review Text:
OUTDOOR ENTERTAINMENT AMONG THE PINES
This open air theater is located inside Peninsula State Park in Fish Creek. If you enter within one hour of the performance and park in the correct parking lot, you don’t have to purchase a park pass. Just driving into the park to find that lot is quite the adventure, following the signs and hoping for the best. The performances throughout the year are varied and I thoroughly enjoyed the one I attended.
Outdoor theater at its best. Not the run-of-the mill plays.  Talented folks.
Entertaining, funny, great production values!
What an amazing setting! The actors were amazing as well.   It is an immaculate venue that is for sure one of a kind.  When you are looking to create memories, this has to be a stop.
My happy place!

We had a wonderful evening seeing the Dairy Heirs! The venue is beautiful and the performance was very high quality and enjoyable! The upgrade for the cushion on your seat was worth it, it was an extremely thick and comfortable cushion. Highly recommend!
A much go to see. It was great, and very entertaining.  Very professional show.
We have never seen a bad show. Ever. Family friendly. Funny. Heartwarming. Every show is packed with these elements and a TON of talent.
The show was fantastic!  Definitely recommend.
Wonderful show and time with friends outdoors!!
Great experience. We were here 2 years ago at the campground and saw a show. Came back to Peninsula SP again and stayed longer so we could see two shows this time. Super fun.

Had a great evening at Northern Sky.  We just happened to be in Door County on opening day (night) of Something in the Water.  An original production just for the outdoor amphitheater.  Very professional show, great rustic ambiance and we had near perfect weather.  I would caution that you should be prepared for the current weather conditions AND possible mosquitoes!!
Any show you see, in the park or their indoor theatre, will be original, warm, and funny. The casts and musicians are so talented. It is one of our favorite places to be.
The shows were so fun!! They're made specifically for this theatre! So the two shows they were offering were well worth it!
Excellent theater production ! Wonderful venue & the singing voices were most impressive ! Clean good Belgian fun !! Really enjoyed it !
What a talented group of actors and musicians. We loved the performance of The Fisherman’s Daughters in the park. Can’t wait to be back next year!
Beyond my expectations! I really felt like the acting and story pulled me in! Enjoyed it immensely. We saw the Fisherman's Daughter. Highly recommend.
Great shows as always
Good family friendly
Amazing outdoor theater in Peninsula State Park with a talented cast. Each performance is a treat!  Northern Sky is a legend in the area. Local storytelling and amazing actors. Highly recommend!

These are such great shows!! We have been coming every year as a family for over a decade. They are fun to watch and the setting in the woods is beautiful. The ticket office is so kind and gave me rain check tickets when I needed to cancel due to a medical problem.
A truly professional theatre among the stars. Most unique, gorgeous, theatre experience offered. Been coming back for over 25 years.

Absolutely a gem of a Wisconsin treasure. This theatre has grown and changed through the years but always stayed true to its populist creed - original musicals based in Wisconsin culture, priced and appropriate for families. Funny, surprising, heartfelt and unique. No one should pass up a chance to see one of their shows. The music is unbelievable and so are the voices!!
Great Wisconsin theater outdoors. Nice seats concession food. Beautiful area. Handicap accessible.
So so beautiful! Best musical under the stars
What a unique venue with really amazing actors. We see at least one show each summer at the outside venue, if not two. Such a wonderful experience.
Absolutely love this place.  Amazing talent. From the writers, production team, musicians and actors!!  Volunteers are friendly and helpful.  We will be back!
Lovely watching live theater in the middle of a forest!
Saw  the play ,Cheeseheads last night. The actors were amazing. The crowd participation was a joy to behold. And the outdoor theater amazing. Can't wait to go back!
It's really fun to see these world original productions. The quality is surprisingly good in this outdoor theater.
Great setting and wonderful creative shows
Folk theatre at it's finest, right in the midst of Peninsula State Park. Fantastic!!
Great summer stock theater that commissions new works with themes related to the local Door County. Cheese heads the Musical was... well... cheesy !! But lots of fun !!
I love coming to these shows! I have been to 6 of them now, and they are all great! The stories are original, set in Wisconsin,  and are very relatable and heartening. You won't regret going!
Wonderful performance. No mosquitoes around me. Very enjoyable time.
Great and unique experience. Very cool location in state park with stage surrounded by tall majestic pines.  Professional lighting and small live orchestra. Performances excellent.

This theater is a unique gem in the woods. If you want to be entertained with great energy, talent, and humor, I highly recommend it. Take the entire family twice, the shows are worth the money and change nightly. This is a priority for me anytime I’m in the area and I love seeing the new shows annually. I’ve never seen a theater and company as neat as this one.

A Door County must! We have taken family and friends every year. This year so far: Fish Whisperer and Cheeseheads! The Musical- both at the outdoor ampitheater. Pure delight (and the space itself- the trees! the stars! the smell of pine! is part of the magic).  Great fun all around.  At the indoor Gould, we've seen Guys On Ice and Guys & Does. Brilliant acting, great voices, heartwarming in every way. Perfect for all ages. Kids especially will love the outdoor theater shows. We call it "theater with heart".
We have so many family memories related to a night at Northern Sky Theater. You can't beat sitting outside under the stars listening to live theater. It's the essence of Door County.

We love Northern Sky Theater. We see their performance every year both indoors and out. We love the outdoor experience in the park but their new indoor theater is beautiful and great in bad weather. All plays are a lot of fun for the entire family!
This place is a gem in the woods. We saw Cheeseheads. Very funny.

So fun!! A little disorganized at the start but we also had a chance of rain so staff was trying to make the best of what could happen.
Definitely recommend and take the whole family!
We will be going up again and making sure this is part of our tradition when visiting door county!
Loved it! The complimentary buf spray was an added bonus since we forgot to bring ours. Very professional actors and actresses.
Funny play.

What a magical theater! They only show new and original works. We saw Cheeseheads and it was a marvelous script, acting and music! We paid more for the cushions and it was worth it. Don't miss a night here!

Always a great place to go for warm and entertainment. Love the outdoor theatre and the cast is always corny, funny, and  a good message in their musical. We have been going for over 20 years and look forward to it as one of our highlights in Door County.
Great people, great theatres and great shows!
Love going!
First time to see a play there. Fantastic!
Absolutely awesome talent with an outdoor setting
Truly one of my happy places. Remarkable theatre!
Cheese Heads was hilarious. I would recommend seeing this show at this magical venue to anyone,

We saw The Fish Whisperer for the first time. Not quite what I expected but it was a very cool show. Can't go wrong with the actors in the cast. It very tastefully dealt with some very serious topics without getting too heavy. Very cute song and dance at the end. (Oops. Maybe I wasn't supposed to give that away!) Can't wait to see it again!
Great time! family friendly shows, everyone should go
Funny, funny, funny funny we go there to all the shows every summer.
Always a great musicals with a local twist. Original writing.
Always fun Door County a great place to live. Wonderful shows n plays
Beautiful setting, plays are getting a little too repetitious in their plot lines.

What a wonderful place to get lost in a theatrical fantasy! As you drive deep into the forest of the Peninsula State Park, it's hard to imagine a theater set outdoors. Once you are seated, it feels like you are in your own little world for awhile. After the show, the darkened paths you entered on are romantically lit so you can safely find your way back to your car and the real world.

Northern Sky is a unique gem of a theater in the middle of the woods. The plays are quirky, fun, and usually have some kind of local angle to them (fictional or historical) always done with excellence.
Being outdoors the weather can be a factor, but even in a light drizzle the audience stayed through the show and the actors showed their appreciation at the end of the night.
What a hidden gem! All original shows in an unforgettable atmosphere. Yes, a little out of the way, but so worth it! Whether you love live performances or new to it, I promise a great time!
Great outdoor shows for the family to see.
A very cool venue to watch a post. We watched Love Stings and it was cute. Fun for the whole family.
Love the plays at Northern Sky. I have seen them since they were the Heritage Ensemble.
Wonderful experience.  Fantastic setting.   Performers are extremely talented.  Great experience!
Beautiful outdoor theater with unique, funny & great musical shows!
Was excellent , we enjoyed it.
If you want a great place to relax and see a great family play this is it.

Some of my favorite Door County experiences have been live performances at Northern Sky Theater. Good comedy loaded with inside jokes (about Wisconsinites). The outdoor setting adds to the experience.
Always amazes me!  Great for couples, families and friends.  Enjoy!
We had such great laughs at the 'Love Stings' performance tonight with a special extra fireside guitar performance afterward. There is nothing like this place, in the woods under the stars. Love it!
Great show & performers!  Loved it!  Can't wait til the next show!  Saw Love Stings tonight.
Owner Response Text: Thanks for the three positive exclamations, Rebecca! See you under the stars soon.

Review Text: Absolutely one of our favorite activities in Door County!!!  We saw Love Stings this week and it was fantastic!!!!  A must see!!!
We saw Dairy Heirs and it was wonderful.  The entire experience exceeded our expectations.  The story, the acting, the singing, and the location were excellent.  Cannot wait to visit again!

Great singing and dancing
Good storytelling
Wonderful outdoor theater with home grown musicals and comedy!
Nice place to be! Its a cool feeling knowing you are watching a live stage performance outside, in a state park, on a penninsula on Lake Michigan.

Super fun experience, I didn’t love the story line (it was kind of lame) but the talent is amazing, the sound and lighting are awesome for being outdoors. It was just a fun experience.

I am docking a star for the handicap driver who mowed over someone’s E-Bike and didn’t bother to pick it up. He knew he did it, looked at it and kept driving. I picked it up.

A very unique experience. This "Theater in the Woods" is literally that. Its in the middle of Peninsula State Park, in a secluded part away from the campsites, so it is far from any light or noise pollution where you get to hear the sounds of the forest and experience the sun vanish as you enjoy VERY WISCONSIN CENTRIC performances. Usually filled with tons of Wisconsin references that most can relate to, the performances are very well done.
It's always a treat to experience talented musicians and actors in original shows in this great outdoor theater in the park!
We've loved every Northern Sky production we've seen! Always entertaining!
Best place ever! Put it on your bucket list!?
Love these original plays.  So much fun!  Thank you!
Loved the play....

Arrived at 7 pm for 7:30 pm performance via uber. Were told box office closed at 7. Get tickets on line. Tried that, but it also stopped at 7.  Tried to see if we could buy a ticket for tomorrow, but watch tonight. People at the theater would not allow.  Now waitng for another uber. Spent $30 in ubers and saw no play. Wont be back

The Northern Sky Theater is an intimate, outdoor venue. The tickets are reasonably priced and the shows I've seen have been fantastic! The amount of talent on the stage is always incredible. The shows are also family friendly. My kids look forward to seeing shows here each summer.
Amazing experience! We go every time we are visiting!

Have been to several performances over the past 5 years. Have always enjoyed the one of a kind plays. The actors are very professional and quite entertaining. Most of the time the actors are the musical players too. The venue is in the woods with the sky above. When going remember to bring bug repellent and a flashlight to see with after the play. Seating is always good and they provide cushions for the wood benches.
Very entertaining play. Very talented group. Amphitheater in the middle of the woods. Would highly recommend.
Worth every penny.  Spend the extra for the cushion.  Excellent!
We have been coming here for a couple of decades because the plays are always wonderful. They are funny, touching, and relatable. Come. You won't be disappointed.
This is such an incredible experience! Plays under the stars in the middle of the woods. A definite must if in Door County.
We saw Tongue in Cheek and it was excellent. We will be going to see Naked Radio later this summer for the second time. Hilarious show.
Absolutely wonderful!  Beautiful night. Outstanding performance!  Thoroughly enjoyable.  We'll be back for the next one.
Very Unique! Good show with a great environment. Also side note... the bathrooms are the best bathrooms i've ever seen at a state park.
What's not to love about great plays under the stars in the park!!!
Saw Tongue in Cheek. Cute show, excellent singers and musicians.
Tongue & Cheek had amazing talent
Fun fun fun!!

Great to experience live theater in the woods once again! "Tongue and Cheek" is a sweet show that's sure to delight the whole family and only an hour long.  Make sure you get your tickets in advance!!
Never gets old!!!
Very entertaining and funny plays
Always a great time. Our family attends every year.
Best example of local theater.  Great shows and talent.
Amazing productions year after year!Love the outdoor theater, and the bugs weren't at all a problem this year! I'd still bring bug spray, just in case.
We saw a very entertaining musical. The setting is beautiful.
Loved the show!
Always a great show!

My father-in-law cries after every play. That's how you know it's good. The venue is unparalleled. You'll never experience a better theatre venue (seat cushions are recommended).  The music is consistently excellent (my favorite part)! The shows are well-performed and well-written. We always make a point to see a play when we come to door county.
We love Northern Sky! My wife and I visit at least once a year and try to catch a production.
So happy they are back.   No food or drink for sale yet.

Experienced opening night of The Fisherman's Daughters.  It was a very fun show and a perfect setting.  We really enjoyed the theater.  Staff were very friendly and welcoming.  Would definitely return when in the area.
Owner Response Text: Hi Nancy, thanks for coming to our opening week! I'm happy to read that you enjoyed it so much that you'll return next time you're in Door County. See you again under the stars!

Review Text: Great play by best actors. This is a surprising venue in the north country. Wonderful experience!
Love love love this theatre and setting. The garden is wonderful and the view of the sunset amazing.
Owner Response Text: Thank you, Nina!  We so appreciate your positive feedback.   We look forward to seeing you in 2026!

Review Text: Saw Steel Magnolias and it made me cry. All those girls did a wonderful job and don't even get me started on the theater! The grounds around the theater are a dream with a beautiful Green Bay view and the theater inside is just beautiful! Everyone working was so kind, the seats are roomy and comfortable, and the performances amazing! I can't wait to have another visit! ❤️

Nighttime 🌌
Magic ✨
Forever 💖

Amazing! The highlight of our vacation in Door County. Walking into the garden from the parking lot felt like being transported to another world, and the sunset was breathtaking. The show, "Dear Jack, Dear Louise" was phenomenal. We will definitely return next time we are in Door County.
Went tonight to see Dear Jack  Dear Loiuse.  The play was excellent.  My husband even enjoyed it.  The theater and grounds are awesome.  Definitely plan to go back soon.
Loved this place! Awesome experience. Totally worth the money.

Such a lovely spot with beautiful grounds, the nicest sunset view over the lake and a big, spacious theatre. Make sure to arrive early to enjoy the space before the play. The performance itself was also great, highly enjoyable!

A great place, the staff is superb, the grounds are beautiful and the production we saw, Little Women, was very well done.  Highly recommend people go early and take advantage of the picnic tables and the surroundings
Owner Response Text:
Thank you so much for the kind words, Denise!  We are all so thankful that you enjoyed your time with us at LITTLE WOMEN THE BROADWAY MUSICAL. Word of mouth is one of our best marketing tools and we so appreciate you taking the time to share your experience. 

My very best,
Brian Kelsey 
Managing Director

Review Text: I saw last night's performance of Little Women. As I write this, the goose bumps come back. I was completely transported to Chicago, LA or New York. The performance was stunning. EVERYONE involved on stage was absolutely top notch in their field. The singing was spectacular, the dancing and the timing were perfection. And to get all of this in a venue like that? Well, pinch me, I can't believe I live in this paradise. The venue has such a welcoming vibe, I love that you can bring your own food and drink and enjoy the beauty before the show (take everything back to your car before the show). I could go every night and never get enough. Thank you Peninsula Players Theatre, your staff, volunteers and everyone that contributes to this fine performance center. Door County is so lucky!
Owner Response Text:
Dear Sandy,

Thank you so much for your glowing review of LITTLE WOMEN THE BROADWAY MUSICAL and your experience whilst at the Theatre!  We so appreciate you sharing your thoughts as word of mouth is our most powerful marketing tool.  

We hope to see you again soon!
My best,
Brian Kelsey
Managing Director

Review Text: Misery is a great show with 3 actors who do a great job.
Owner Response Text:
Thank you for the wonderful feedback, Fred!  We are thrilled you enjoyed the show and the dedicated work of the acting company as they navigate this complicated and demanding production!    We look forward to you joining us again soon!  

My best,
Brian Kelsey
Managing Director

Review Text: Highlight of our trip to Door County
Owner Response Text:
Hello JoAnn,

We are so happy that you joined us and that we will be a memorable part of your Door County vacation!  We hope you will join us again the next time you visit!

My best,

Brian Kelsey
Managing Director

Review Text:
All around GREAT experience
From purchasing tickets to viewing the actual play, Peninsula Players staff made it an easy & seamless process!

I appreciated the informative email ahead of time so that I knew what to expect with the electronic tickets.

There were plenty of staff making the parking an easy experience. The grounds of the theatre are absolutely GORGEOUS, we enjoyed all of the benches & places to sit in various locations. Loooved the beer garden area & the bartender had a GREAT story.

Throughout the entire evening, we found plenty of smiling & informative staff to help us constantly! It was great! I’m not sure we’ve ever gone to a theatre with staff that was so kind! Clearly they love their jobs!

The actual theatre is spectacular—comfy seats with plenty of legroom & excellent acoustics! I have a hearing impairment and I had ZERO issues there!

Because of this wonderful experience, we’ve now purchased tickets to 2 more shows this summer!

Thank you, Peninsula Players for providing us such a great experience! We can’t wait to come back!
Owner Response Text:
Dear Lauri,

Thank you very much for taking the time to share your experience!  I couldn’t be more proud of the entire team for delivering on the Peninsula Players Theatre experience from the ticketing process, the parking and Beer Garden, as well as your enjoyment of our facility and productions. Thank you for taking the time to write such a wonderful review. We look forward to welcoming you back again soon to our home down by the bay!

My very best,
Brian Kelsey
Managing Director

Review Text: Always a pleasure to visit this theatre. The venue can’t be beat! It’s always such a special experience.
Owner Response Text:
Thank you for sharing your experience, Emma!  I am so pleased that you have included us as part of your summer Door County plans!  We look forward to welcoming you back again soon!

My best,
Brian Kelsey
Managing Director

Review Text: Professional performances in a fun Agatha Christie play were supported by an outstanding crew that made this a very enjoyable afternoon. The setting is beautiful and the facilities surpassed our expectations. Our only disappointment was the limited offerings in the Cantina - bring your own lunch.
Excellent venue! The play was great and we enjoyed an awesome sunset, a bon fire and some cocktails before the play in thier outdoor gathering area.
Nice experience. Laid back with beautiful views of the setting sun. Nice theater.

First time visiting Door County and so glad we went to the Peninsula Players Theatre. The setting and view of the lake are beautiful, and the openness of the theater was lovely. The staff made it easy to park, and the cast of The Angel Next Door were amazing! Honestly the talent level is equal to shows I’ve seen on Broadway and big city theaters, and the atmosphere and location can’t be beat. One of the highlights of our trip!
The actors were great again. Keep up the great job.
Owner Response Text: Thank you for the kind words, Sherrie!  Thank you for joining us!

Review Text: What a fun experience!  A unique location where you can enjoy a drink in the beer garden right on the lake pre-show and explore the nature.  The theater itself is beautiful and of course all the shows are great.  A must visit while you are in the area🙂
Owner Response Text: Thank you for joining us and taking the time to share your experience, Joey!  We hope to see you again soon!

Review Text: If your in the Door County area, you must watch a play at The Penninsula Players Theatre! Great shows, great views, and great atmosphere!
Owner Response Text: Thank you for the kind words and recommendation, Amy!

Review Text: Great venue

I've been to Peninsula Players many times, and have seen a wide variety of shows. Many have been fabulous, including "Always, Patsy Cline" and "The Full Monty."

I love going early and watching the sun set on the lake, having a glass of wine in the garden, and chatting with others in the theater. It's so. . . Door County! Truly one of my favorite theaters.

Insider tips:  Wear sensible shoes because you'll probably find yourself walking on uneven surfaces. Dress for the weather, and be prepared for the theater to cool off when the sun goes down. No one will think it's odd if you bring in your car blanket on a chilly fall evening. If you want, you can bring a picnic and enjoy it beforehand, but remember that you absolutely may not carry in alcoholic beverages. And nothing but water is allowed inside the theater.

My wife and I always enjoy attending a play at Peninsula Players Theatre. The staff is outstanding, the location is perfect, and the grounds and atmosphere are wonderful. There isn't a bad seat in the house. Try PP the next time you are in Door County.
Loved it fantastically funny play.
My wife and I attended a play during our honeymoon in Door County! Great play and atmosphere.

Definitely a wonderful experience.  The outdoor garden area and the sunset over the water is very relaxing and  beautiful. The play was very entertaining.  The actors did such a great job. Great mystery and funny.
Great performance
Absolutely incredible!
Incredible venue, great actors and plays.

We have attended many performances at this venue over the last 30 years, and are now season ticket holders, having moved here 2 years ago.
The breadth of offerings and the quality of the productions is second to none.
The current show, Trying, is just snother example of how this theater engages, challenges  and entertain s its audiences.
Do not hesitate to ser this, or any show they do. The hroundsand views  are soectacular and the art is equally so

We have seen half a dozen shows over the past 20 years.  They have All been great!  The grounds and shore are nice and relaxing to enjoy before the show as well as during the intermission bonfire.  Don't take my word, go to a show and experience it for yourself.  You can thank me later!
Owner Response Text: Nate, thank you for spending time with us over the years and sharing your experience!  We look forward to your next visit.

Review Text: A fun place to go to the theatre. Just make sure to bring a jacket/blanket as the side panels stay open and it gets chilly.
Wow.  What a special place with special people and a special performance!  A visit to Door County will never be complete without a show.  Wow!
Owner Response Text: Thank you so much for your kind comments, Garon!  We are so happy that you enjoyed your visit to our home down by the bay and we hope to see you again soon!

Review Text: A real thinker performance, thought provoking.
Owner Response Text: Thank you for joining us, Carol!  This show definitely makes us ask our own questions about what it is we know, believe, and hold near to us.

Review Text: Not my cup of tea. But the grounds and location are wonderful and worth a look.

Wonderful live professional Theater Group performances by very talented  actors.  A Door County staple.  Theater is in a barn type theater with ample seating.  Drafty so dress warmly.  Many in audience brought lap blankets.

We saw"Muder for Two"--two actors perform all the roles.
Kind of slapstick dinner theater fare...silly, fun, entertaining. Definitely NOT for the high brow playgoer.  We enjoyed it.
Great plays and the play house is wonderful.  My wife and I enjoyed the play and when my kids are older I will take them there as well.
Owner Response Text:
Dear Sean,  thank you for the kind words!  We are so pleased that you have had a positive experience and we look forward to the day when you may introduce your children to the Peninsula Players Theatre experience.

We hope to see you soon!

Review Text: Absolutely amazing. Excellent production. Actors were phenomenal. This was our first time at Peninsula Players Theater and we were happy to have finally made it there. We definately will be back. Kudos!!!!!
Owner Response Text: Thank you, Gale!  We are so happy your first experience was a memorable one!  We hope to see you by the bay again soon!

Review Text: Absolutely beautiful venue. It's an outdoor theater so dress accordingly. The grounds are well maintained and landscaped with plenty of seating and it's right on the water. The only reason I'm deducting a point is that they had a Fall Special hot apple cider with Captain Morgan and they ran out of Captain in the first 1/2 hour of opening. It was a cold evening and it should it would have been nice.
Third year in a row, play was great. Theater is really nice.

Review Text:
Third Avenue Playhouse is a professional theater in a small midwestern  venue. The performances are always top notch with riveting performances by the carefully selected actors. The sets always add so much to the experience as well.
The staff led by Jacob Janssen strive to make this a community theater for everyone to enjoy, with “pay what you will “ on dress rehearsal nights or even checking out “gratis” tickets from the local library.
You gotta check it out.

Review Text: Here for "Ryan's Pub: Trivia Night" with Doug Mancheski and Kelli Strickland. World premiere of highly unusual two-person hyper speed dialogue drama with a breathless script that defies the imagination. (How do they do that? You ask yourself.) A truly amazing performance by both actors, but Kelli steals the show. Alex Polzin deserves kudos for the scene design. A real accomplishment. TAP is showing its stuff. Fortunate to have this company in town.
Owner Response Text: Thank you for sharing your experience, Joe!

Review Text: Two great actors!  The characters of Richie & Marcie developed their dynamic and loyal relationship from the very start of the play.  The setting was so organic to their life - the dirt foundation, duct taped booths and jukebox player - their deep attention to each other during their individual expression was intimate to their firm yet frail grasp on their circumstances.  Thank you for this unique perspective!
Owner Response Text: We're so glad you enjoyed the world premiere of Ryan's Pub, Trivia Night! Thank you for supporting our theatre.

Review Text: Always interesting plays with minimal actors sometimes playing multiple roles. Sure keeps it interesting as they also move props around. Bought season tickets so I don't miss a performance!
Owner Response Text: Thank you for your support, Kathleen!

Review Text:
We just saw *39 Steps* at Third Avenue Playworks (TAP) and… WOW. What an absolute blast! From the moment the lights came up, the physical antics of the cast had us laughing non-stop.

How *four* incredibly talented performers managed to play so many characters, switch costumes in a heartbeat, and move set pieces with such perfect timing is mind-blowing. The pace was so fast and the energy so high that we were practically exhausted just from laughing—and from watching the cast dash, spin, and transform right before our eyes.

It’s smart, it’s silly, it’s brilliantly executed, and it’s the kind of theater magic that reminds you why live performances are so special. Bravo to TAP for delivering a show that’s as clever as it is hilarious!
Owner Response Text: Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts, David! We're so glad you enjoyed the show.

Review Text: We saw 39 Steps this evening and it was fabulous.  Fast paced and very funny!
Owner Response Text: Thank you for letting us know you enjoyed the show!

Review Text: Absolutely amazing performance! I really didn't know what we were in store for. Maybe a decent play but kind of "cheesy" because the tickets were super affordable....boy I was wrong! The performance was incredible.  The singing was beautiful and the acting was very professional. It was worth every penny spent and more! Take my money! The plays here are so great! Would highly recommend for a date night or a family outing.

My husband and I needed something to do for the third night in Door County. We needed some entertainment. We got last minute tickets for The Mystery of Irma Vep and we had the best experience. Every member of the staff was wonderfully friendly and the play was hilarious. The two actresses were fabulous. We laughed harder than we had in a long time. I recommend that anyone who has a free night in Door County go see a performance at the TAP!! I will make this a priority on my next trip.

Review Text: Better than theater in Chicago and L.A.!!! If your are reading this and The Mystery of Irma Vep is still playing, it is a MUST see!!! It is hilarious, fun, and we are still laughing recalling the rapid fire, quick change, energy packed production we saw! The actors Karen Estrada and Natalie Rich should be on Broadway, yes they are THAT GREAT!!! This is a gem of a place and I am sure no matter what they are going to produce next will be as awesome.  No matter where you are staying in Door County or anywhere nearby, you need to know you have some extreme talent right there in Sturgeon Bay!!! To my friends in the Chicagoland area, this show, and any show they put on will be top notch, and worthy planning your entire stay around.  We will be back, and we will look forward to visiting this very cosmopolitan production company again and again!!! Highly recommend !!!
Owner Response Text: Lawrn, what a fantastic review! Thank you!!

The show, Daddy Long Legs, was opening that evening, so we purchased tickets.  What a treat!! Fantastic production worthy of any run in Chicago or even NY.  The theater is in a new beautiful space, the people working and attending charming.  Highly recommend that if there is a play while you are in Door County, you try to attend.
nice cozy theater with good acoustics

We had been coming to Door County for close to 30 years and had always enjoyed various county theatre productions but had never gone to TAP. Sturgeon Bay seemed far away from where we stayed, so we never made the effort to spend time in the city.
That all changed when we moved to the county full time in the summer of 2021.
We became  subscribers in 2022 and thoroughly enjoyed the seasons' diverse offerings.  We did not hesitate to renew for 2023 as soon as  subscriptions opened.
Last night we saw the world premiere of I Carry Your Heart With Me and it was an incredible experience.
We sat transfixed for the short (65 minute) but powerful one character play. Karen Estrada had command of the stage, taking us through a wide range of emotions and delivering a fantastic performance.
It was, quite simply, one of the best, most engaging, and thought-provoking night of theatre we have ever seen.
If you have the opportunity, go see this show or any show that TAP offers. If you don't have the opportunity, you will be missing out on a first-rate theatre experience.

My wife and I recently attended a fantastic radio play of "A Christmas Carol" at the TAP. It was a great show and a very pleasant experience overall.  I would not hesitate to attend a future production at this venue. Kudos!

Its a performance space for everyone to enjoy. Now go!

Review Text: We rented the theater for our wedding ceremony and the backstage for our reception. They let us set up early, which I did not expect at all and it made things very easy. Samantha listened to our ideas and made them all possible. The space is beautiful and historic and we were so fortunate to be able to have our wedding there. It was very easy to work with their AV technician for sound during the ceremony. Backstage was perfect and was able to transform from a seated dinner to an amazing dance floor. The accommodations were amazing. The only thing I would retract from was a couple of mix ups with dietary restrictions. One of my guests with a nut allergy was given a plate with nuts, and then the dietary restriction meals came around, after she already ate the plate containing nuts. This guest ended up having to go to the emergency room. Backstage did remove the cost of that plate from our invoice, but I wish the mix up did not happen in the first place.
It is a beautiful theater and was an awesome venue for the wedding we attended!

I played violin for a beautiful wedding on the Meyer Theatre stage a couple days ago. The setting was unique and elegant and the staff was extremely helpful and professional. The reception was held in the lovely backstage room which was very convenient for the guests.
The Meyer Theatre is an absolutely amazing venue for a wedding.
Owner Response Text: Love to hear this! Thanks so much, Karen!

Review Text: Beautiful atmosphere for small performances of major artists.
Such a beautiful venue!

It has a good atmosphere with the sky painting an design around the theater.  But you can see most noticeably the chipping paint and material around the front ceiling of the theater it badly needs to be re-done.

It's frustrating trying to buy tickets for any shows.  There's no way to choose seating and if you want to sit together with someone the seating is deceiving for example row K seats 1 and 3.  A normal person would assume there's an empty seat between these two no they're next to each other.  The seats themselves are all very visible to the stage which is a plus but they're small and cramped.  I was not comfortable at all.

The staff who direct people to their seats really need to replace their standard white flash light with a red or blue light.  Especially after the show starts.  Several times the usher shined his light in my eyes while trying to show others to their seat after the show had started.

Their concession line for alcohol was very long and most people were standing in line on the stairs, how is this safe?

I've not been to this theater much in the past but I recently went to Hard Bodies the show was poorly run with the director constantly have contestants come onto stage then having them go back and then calling them back out for rewards.  Having people during people's demonstrations running on and off stage to get the judges food and beverages.  Miss Green Bay who's talent was supposed to be violin missing several notes making me cringe during the national anthem. The comedian Steve Treviño who started off with this other guy who made tasteless racist jokes and the show which itself started almost 10 minutes late.  Treviño was funny the the organization of this theater is horrible.
Beautiful venue. Every seat is good.

Great seating due to being a smaller venue.  It would be nice if people would stay seated for most of the performance. I understand it is fun to stand once in awhile but people pay to "see" the show, not just listen to it! Maybe there could be an announcement at the start to ask people to remain seated for most of the show. Not trying to be a prude. I love to stand like everyone else but this isn't the venue for it.

I dressed in jeans and a t-shirt.  The air was uncomfortably warm and stagnant. Viewer's were fanning themselves. The staff has little control over people staying seated and socializing while music is playing. The beer and snack selections are limited, expensive and beer is not very cold. I broke away from the poorly mixed sound, you couldn't make out the lyrics, to buy beer. Their were only four customers ahead of me and it took over 10 minutes to get the beer. That was with two staff selling and 1 stocking the not very cold coolers. No comparison at all with the professional experience at the Manitowoc Capital Theatre.

We were expecting to see a beautiful celebration to honor the birth of our savior, Jesus Christ. A mockery of our Lord Jesus was apparent. One of the commentators even referred to Jesus as a brat. We spent over 150.00 on these tickets and left as soon as the drapes closed for a break. The music was not familiar Christmas Carols. If you are a Christian do not waste your time and money.  I would identify it as sac-religious. To refer to the christ child as a brat was enough. They did not show the manger or respecting his birth in ANY WAY. Ruined our family  tradition to honor the birth of our Jesus Christ. IF YOU HONOR OUR LORD JESUS CHRIST, do not attend thus event!!!!
Owner Response Text: Thanks for your input, Patricia. We will be sure to pass this on to the show's producer.
